title: Biweekly change in confirmed COVID-19 cases
subtitle: The biweekly change on any given date measures the percentage change in the number of new confirmed cases over the last 14 days, relative to the number in the previous 14 days. Due to limited testing, the number of confirmed cases is lower than the true number of infections.
caption:
Biweekly change in confirmed COVID-19 cases

The biweekly change on any given date measures the percentage change in the number of new confirmed cases over the last 14 days, relative to the number in the previous 14 days. Due to limited testing, the number of confirmed cases is lower than the true number of infections.

The world map displays the biweekly change in confirmed COVID-19 cases as of February 22, 2026, using a color scale ranging from dark blue (-100%) to dark red (+100%), with hatched areas indicating no data. The vast majority of the globe, including North America, Africa, Asia, and Australia, is hatched, signifying an absence of reported data for this metric. In South America, Peru is shaded in the darkest blue band, representing a change between -100% and -75%. Neighboring Ecuador and Uruguay fall within the -75% to -50% range, while Argentina and Chile are situated in the -50% to -25% band. Brazil and Colombia appear in the lightest blue shade, corresponding to the -25% to 0% interval. In Europe, a cluster of countries including Spain, France, and Italy are colored in the lightest blue, indicating a decrease between 0% and -25%. Conversely, Sweden and Finland are shaded in light orange, placing them in the 0% to 25% increase band, while Norway sits near the boundary of the 25% to 50% range. Iceland is the only nation visible in the darker orange bands, suggesting an increase between 50% and 75%. Small island nations in the Caribbean also show variation, with some appearing in the darkest blue band and others in the lightest blue.
